differences.detail: Production is a narrow mobile single-column flow with compact vertical spacing; prototype is a wide desktop modal with much larger paddings and panel gaps.
Production uses smaller, denser text and tighter stat labels; prototype uses larger headers/body text and looser typographic rhythm.
Production leans navy/blue with restrained accent chips; prototype introduces stronger red emphasis (CTA/follow) and brighter green highlights, shifting visual balance.
Production cards/chips are moderately rounded; prototype uses softer/larger rounding on modal and containers, giving a different component feel.
Production effects are subtle; prototype has stronger glow/blur and halo lighting, increasing perceived depth and reducing parity.
Production keeps portrait/hero visuals relatively compact within dense lists; prototype uses a much larger hero portrait and larger hero tiles, changing weight distribution.
Production prioritizes team overview + lineup + long-scroll detail; prototype prioritizes player modal storytelling with prominent bottom CTA, so primary attention order differs.

top_3_fixes: Match production layout model first: mobile single-column sheet, full-width cards, and tighter spacing scale (roughly 12-20px rhythm).
Normalize typography to production density: reduce heading/body sizes and tighten stat/label spacing so more information sits above the fold.
Align visual tokens: reduce glow intensity, shrink major border radii, and rebalance accents toward production’s subdued navy/cyan/yellow with less dominant red CTA treatment.
